---
title: Exercises
number-sections: true
---

## Basic App {#ex-basic-app}

::: {.exercise}
Open `code/demos/app-01-intro.py` file in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/demos) and run it.

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

Try out any one of the following changes:

1. Add a third input — a checkbox group to filter by species — and connect it to the plot
2. Try `ui.input_slider()` to filter by one of the numeric columns
3. Add a `@render.text` output showing how many points are currently displayed
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution — species checkbox
```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex01a-species_checkbox.py >}}
```

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution — body mass slider



```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex01b-slider.py >}}
```

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution — full solution (checkbox + count)

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex01c-full.py >}}
```

:::

## Reactivity {#ex-reactivity}

::: {.exercise}

**Part A** — open `code/demos/app-03-reactivity.py` file in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/demos).

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

1. Run it and confirm the scatter plot and count update together
2. Add a `print("filtering...")` inside `filtered()` — change Species and verify it prints only once

**Part B** — open `code/demos/app-04-reactivity_chained.py` file in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/demos).

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

1. Run it and explore all three outputs (plot, count, table)
2. Change Y Axis — notice count does *not* change (filtered is still cached)
3. Add a `print` to each calc to confirm which ones re-run for each input change
:::

## Tips {#ex-tips}

::: {.exercise}

Take a look at the `code/exercise/app-ex03-tips.py` file in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/exercises).

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

1. Replace the plotly scatter plot with the altair scatter plot below.
2. Wrap the scatter plot in a function, `scatterplot()`,
3. Wse the appropriate output component decorator, `@render_altair`

```{.python}
data = ____ # use the reactive calc dataframe here
points = alt.Chart(data).mark_circle().encode(
    x=alt.X("total_bill", title="Total bill"),
    y=alt.Y("tip", title="Tip"),
    tooltip=[
        alt.Tooltip("total_bill:Q", title="Total bill", format=".2f"),
        alt.Tooltip("tip:Q", title="Tip", format=".2f"),
    ],
)
trend = points.transform_regression(
    "total_bill", "tip", method="linear"
).mark_line()
zoom = alt.selection_interval(bind="scales")
return (points + trend).add_params(zoom).properties(width="container")
```

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex03-tips.py >}}
```

:::

## Tips + Button {#ex-tips-button}

::: {.exercise}

Take a look at the `code/exercises/app-ex03-tips.py` file in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/exercises).

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

1. Add a `ui.input_action_button()` component for the action button.
    - You can use the `"action_button"` id.
2. Create a `reset_filters()` function that uses the `ui.update_slider()` and `ui.update_checkbox_group()` functions to reset the filter values to their default state.

```python
def reset_filters():
    ui.update_slider(
        "slider",
        value=[tips.total_bill.min(), tips.total_bill.max()],
    )
    ui.update_checkbox_group(
        "checkbox_group",
        selected=["Lunch", "Dinner"],
    )
```

3. Decorate the function with a `@reactive.event(input.action_button)` call.
4. Decorate the function __again__ with a `@reactive.effect` call.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex04-button.py >}}
```

:::

## Refactor into a Module {#ex-modules-refactor}

::: {.exercise}

Take a look at `code/exercises/app-ex06-modules_refactor.py` in the
[repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/exercises) —
it's the tips dashboard from the Tips + Button exercise, filter sidebar and all.

Before doing the exercise make a copy of the file for your solution.

1. Extract the sidebar's `ui.input_slider()`, `ui.input_checkbox_group()`,
   and the `@reactive.calc` that filters `tips` into a module named
   `filter_panel` (Express) with signature
   `filter_panel(input, output, session, data, columns)`, returning
   `{"mask": <reactive.calc>}`.
2. Replace the sidebar's filter UI with `filter_panel("tips_filter", data=tips, columns=["total_bill", "time"])`.
3. Keep the "Reset filter" action button working — remember the module's
   inputs are namespaced, so their ids from outside the module are
   `"tips_filter-filter_total_bill"` and `"tips_filter-filter_time"`.
4. Simplify the body down to a single `ui.output_data_frame` showing the
   filtered rows — drop the value boxes, the scatter plot, and the
   ridgeplot from the original. This exercise is about the module
   refactor, not preserving every dashboard widget.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex06-modules_refactor.py >}}

## file: module.py
{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/module.py >}}
```

:::

## Reuse Your Module {#ex-modules-compose}

::: {.exercise}

Start from your Refactor into a Module solution
(`code/exercise_solutions/app-ex06-modules_refactor.py`).

1. Add a `ui.navset_card_tab()` with two `ui.nav_panel()`s: "Tips" (your
   existing content) and "Penguins" (new).
2. In the "Penguins" panel, load `palmerpenguins.load_penguins().dropna()`
   and call `filter_panel("penguins_filter", data=penguins, columns=["species", "bill_length_mm", "body_mass_g"])` —
   no new filter code needed.
3. Add a `ui.output_data_frame` in each panel showing the filtered rows for
   that panel's dataset.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [editor, viewer]
#| layout: vertical
#| viewerHeight: 650

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex07-modules_compose.py >}}

## file: module.py
{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/module.py >}}
```

:::

## Testing {#ex-testing}

::: {.exercise}

Look at the pattern in `code/demos/testing/app.py` and `test_app.py` in the
[repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/demos/testing) —
it uses `run_shiny_app()` plus Shiny's Playwright `controller`s to drive a
running app from a test.

Write a `test_app.py` for your Tips + Button solution
(`code/exercise_solutions/app-ex04-button.py`):

1. Confirm the default state — the `"checkbox_group"` checkboxes start
   selected on `["Lunch", "Dinner"]` and `total_tippers` shows the full row
   count.
2. Change `"checkbox_group"` to only `["Dinner"]` and confirm `total_tippers`
   updates to the filtered count.
3. Click the `"action_button"` ("Reset filter") and confirm
   `"checkbox_group"` and `total_tippers` return to their defaults.

Run it with `pytest`.

:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

Playwright tests drive a real browser — they won't run inside shinylive.
Run locally with `pytest`.

```{.python}
{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/test_app-ex04-button.py >}}
```

:::

## Deploy Your App {#ex-deploy}

::: {.exercise}

1. Create a new GitHub repository
2. Copy your first app code into an `app.py` file
3. Make sure your repository has a `requirements.txt` file
4. Push the `app.py` and `requirements.txt` to GitHub
5. Deploy it to Posit Connect Cloud

<https://docs.posit.co/connect-cloud/how-to/python/shiny-python.html>

:::

## 🎁 Bonus: AI Dashboards {#ex-ai-dashboards}

Let's start from the solution in the previous exercise
`code/exercise_solutions/app-ex04-button.py`.
You can find the original code in the [repository](https://github.com/chendaniely/scipy-2026-shiny/tree/main/code/exercise_solutions).

We are going to convert this app from having multiple filters into an AI Chatbot using `querychat`.

1. Import the querychat library and create the `QueryChat` object using the existing `tips` dataset

```python
from querychat.express import QueryChat

qc = QueryChat(tips, "tips", client="github/gpt-4.1")
```

2. Replace all the sidebar UI elements with the filters (including button) with the querychat sidebar

```python
qc.sidebar()
```

3. Finally, replace the filtered dataframe object with the filtered dataframe returned from querychat

```python
qc.df()
```

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Solution

Will not work in shinylive, run locally.

```{.python}

{{< include /../code/exercise_solutions/app-ex05-querychat.py >}}
```

:::
